food_subgroup: Filled baguette / sandwich with stir-fried meat

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for bánh mì or Vietnamese stir-fried beef baguette sandwich; closest_parent 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_00002473) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sandwiches and filled rolls) is the closest defensible parent category for bánh mì bò xào; no specific Vietnamese sandwich code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for bánh mì bò xào or Vietnamese stir-fried beef sandwich.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bánh mì bò xào sandwich weighing approximately 220 g, consisting of a Vietnamese baguette filled with stir-fried beef, pickled vegetables, cucumber, fresh coriander, and chilli.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated nutrient profile derived from USDA FoodData Central component foods: beef stir-fry (USDA SR Legacy #13365 and related entries), French baguette (USDA SR Legacy #18029), and pickled vegetable garnish components; cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Vietnamese dishes.
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for bánh mì bò xào exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for stir-fried beef, Vietnamese-style baguette, and pickled vegetable garnish, weighted by approximate recipe proportions (baguette ~40%, stir-fried beef ~45%, vegetables/condiments ~15%). FAO/INFOODS SEA data used for cross-validation of Vietnamese beef stir-fry macros.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread / baguette-type products and mixed beef-vegetable dishes; analogous estimate based on French baguette GI (GI=75) and stir-fried beef (GI~0 glycemic contribution), weighted composite for filled sandwich.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bánh mì bò xào. The Vietnamese baguette is the primary glycemic driver (GI ~72–75 for white baguette per University of Sydney GI Database). Stir-fried beef contributes negligible glycemic load. Pickled vegetables and fat content moderate the overall GI. Composite GI estimated at 62 (medium) using the weighted carbohydrate contribution method: baguette carbohydrate fraction ~80% of total carbohydrate × GI 75, plus vegetable fraction ~20% × GI 15, yielding weighted GI ≈ 63, rounded to 62. Glycemic load per 220 g serving = (62 × 23.5 × 2.20) / 100 = 32.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1056 mg per serving) from fish sauce, soy sauce, and oyster sauce used in the stir-fry marinade
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of bánh mì bò xào due to its high sodium load (~1056 mg per standard serving), which represents approximately 46% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; requesting reduced-sauce preparation or limiting portion size is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~62) driven by the white wheat baguette, with moderate carbohydrate content (~23.5 g per 100 g; ~51.7 g per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the white baguette component raises the glycemic index to approximately 62 (medium) with a glycemic load of ~32 per serving; pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables, choosing a smaller portion, or substituting a wholegrain roll where available can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.1 g per 100 g) and high sodium (~480 mg per 100 g) from the stir-fried beef and condiment-heavy marinade
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of moderate saturated fat and high sodium in bánh mì bò xào warrants portion control and infrequent consumption; choosing leaner cuts of beef and requesting reduced fish sauce and soy sauce in preparation can meaningfully lower both saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh mì bò xào is a Vietnamese stir-fried beef baguette sandwich, a popular street-food and café dish originating in Vietnam and widely served in Vietnamese restaurants across Singapore. The dish consists of a crisp, airy Vietnamese-style baguette — a legacy of French colonial influence — split open and filled with wok-tossed sliced beef that has been marinated and stir-fried with garlic, lemongrass, fish sauce, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. The filling is complemented by pickled daikon and carrot (đồ chua), fresh cucumber slices, sprigs of coriander (cilantro), and sliced chilli, creating a balance of savoury, tangy, herbaceous, and spicy flavours. The baguette provides a light crunch on the outside with a soft interior, while the stir-fried beef delivers umami depth and a slightly caramelised char from high-heat wok cooking. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy mixed meal (~198 kcal per 100 g) providing meaningful protein (~11.5 g per 100 g) from beef, moderate carbohydrate (~23.5 g per 100 g) from the baguette, and moderate fat (~6.8 g per 100 g). Sodium content is notably high (~480 mg per 100 g) due to the condiment-heavy marinade, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 62 (medium category), driven primarily by the white wheat baguette. In Singapore, this dish is commonly found in Vietnamese restaurants and food courts, priced around SGD 12–13 per serving. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or soy.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for bánh mì bò xào was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables. Nutrient values were estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for stir-fried beef, French baguette, and pickled vegetable garnish, weighted by approximate recipe proportions. GI was estimated using the weighted carbohydrate contribution method anchored to University of Sydney GI Database values for white baguette. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy or FAO/INFOODS SEA source data for this dish or its component foods. All other micronutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data. Sodium value (~480 mg per 100 g) reflects typical Vietnamese restaurant preparation with fish sauce, soy sauce, and oyster sauce; home or reduced-sodium preparations may differ significantly. The dish is classified as Vietnamese cuisine served in a Singapore context (GrabFood Singapore, Thien Long Vietnam Restaurant). Halal status is marked false as the dish contains beef from a Vietnamese restaurant context where halal certification is not confirmed; consumers requiring halal-certified food should verify with the restaurant. GI estimated at 62 using weighted carbohydrate contribution method: baguette fraction (80% of carbohydrate) × GI 75 + vegetable fraction (20%) × GI 15 = 63, adjusted to 62 to account for fat and protein moderating effect. Glycemic load per 220 g serving = (62 × 23.5 × 2.20) / 100 ≈ 32.2 g.
